notes: Lesson 12 orientation (act 72), 1225.66s, 7 route-aligned content cues re-placed from the real spiral overview transcript (High-level map / Analogy / Tiny example / Mechanism / Implementation / Misconception / Synthesis), specialized to the actual tokenization content and reusing the transformer.py idiom lib (token_row, id_boxes, embedding_table, vector_strip). Running example 'she saw the saw' -> [12,44,91,44] (continuity with Lesson 7). Two review iterations: round-1 READ start/mid/end for all 7 cues and caught (1) Cue0 end: the green 'this table is what the transformer processes' line collided with the token chips; (2) Cue4 MATH ERROR: encode('sea') was [4,1,2] but with the index table s=4,e=2,a=1 it must be [4,2,1] (decode too); (3) Cue4 end: the 'small inputs / named variables' note collided with the collect/sort row; (4) Cue5: the 'four tempting mix-ups' label overlapped the top pair row; (5) Cue6: the perspective-card titles were the same color as their box fill so they were invisible when lit, and the solid fills were loud. Round-2 fixed all five (fade the top pipeline before the closing note in Cue0/Cue4, correct the encode/decode to [4,2,1], lower the Cue5 rows, white INK card titles + subtle-outline lit/dim in Cue6), re-rendered chunks 0/4/5/6, and re-READ them: all pass. Per-cue checklist (formula/label legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches the beat narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Note: cue03/04/05 'end' frames are sampled exactly at the cue boundary and show the next cue's opening title (documented cumulative chunk-pinning artifact); each cue's true final state was validated by the manim -s last-frame guard render. Final -ql 1225.603s vs audio 1225.66s (Δ0.057s). Per-cue: Cue0: 'she saw the saw' -> token pieces -> IDs [12,44,91,44] ('saw' twice -> same 44) -> embedding rows -> '+ position -> a table of numbers'; closing beat fades the tokenizer half and names the payoff table. | Cue1: the pipeline as a workshop line: input object -> Tokenizer(text->IDs) -> Embedding(IDs->rows) -> +Position(rows->table), each station stamps a receipt; tags name the stations. | Cue2: follow one concrete input; the two 'saw' pieces map to the same ID 44 and both point at ONE learned embedding row. | Cue3: id 44 is a row index into the embedding matrix (amber row highlight); the row's numbers carry meaning; sequence length is a real compute cost (attention ~ L^2 with L=4/8/16 bars). | Cue4: build a char vocab from 'she saw' (collect unique -> sort -> char->index table 0..5), encode('sea')=[4,2,1], decode([4,2,1])='sea', assert decode(encode(x))==x. Math verified correct. | Cue5: four tempting mix-ups as A != B (a token != a word, an ID != its vector, a score != a probability, a phase != the operation), revealed one at a time with a per-pair why-note. | Cue6: the pipeline map returns (text->tokens->IDs->table) and five perspective cards (Audio/Visual/Text/Code/Assess) light one at a time; white titles legible on both dim and lit states.
